地址: It's a journey to central Shan East in Huangpur District, Shanghai.
景区分类: Historical and cultural neighbourhoods, urban landscapes
附近交通: Subway Line 2, Nanking East Road Station, Line 10
购票方式: No tickets. Some tourist projects need separate tickets.
景区介绍: One of the most symbolic sites of Shanghai cities is the beach on the side of the Huangpo River, located in central Shanghai City. One and a half kilometres long, south of Yanan East Road, north of the white bridge on the Suzhou River, east of the Huangpur River and west of the former Shanghai financial and foreign trade institutions. The outer beach is home to 52 classical revival buildings of varying styles, known as the All-China Architectural Expo, which is one of the most important landmarks in China’s near-modern history and representative architecture.
适合人群: Family, couples, historical and cultural lovers, photographers
景区卖点: Iconic riverfront skyline, Historic colonial architecture, Stunning night views, Cultural heritage landmark, Vibrant river cruise experience

门票价格: 40 for adults and 20 for children per year (1 April to 30 June and 1 September to 30 November); 30 for adults and 15 for children during the off season (1 December to 31 March and 1 July to 31 August)
购票方式: It can be purchased through an official tender, a live ticket window or a third-party tourism platform
景区介绍: It is located in the north-east of the old town of Shanghai, north by Fukuo Road, east by Anjin Street, south-west by the old town of Anjin Temple, and south-west by association with the Old City of Arguments, and is an ancient garden of Gangnam, built in Ming Dynasty and covering more than 30 acres throughout the calendar year. The park is home to the three stone-named Jade Linglings of Gangnam, the command post of the 1853 Knife uprising, and tourist attractions such as temples and shop streets. It was opened to the public in 1961 and was designated by the State Council in 1982 as the national focal point for the protection of cultural objects.
适合人群: Family visitors, cultural lovers, photographers, historical researchers
景区卖点: Ancient classical garden with unique rock formations; Rich cultural heritage and historical sites; Traditional Chinese architecture and landscaping; Perfect place to experience local Shanghai culture; Beautiful lantern displays during festivals

景区介绍: One of the iconic cultural landscapes of Shanghai is the Middle East Radio and Television Tower, which is approximately 468 metres high and bordered by the Huang Po River and looks beyond the beach. Construction began on 30 July 1991 and was officially opened to the public on 18 November 1994. It has landscapes and facilities such as capsules, rotary restaurants, Shanghai City Historical Development Museums, which are multi-purpose tourist complexes such as city tours, fashion restaurants, shopping and entertainment, historical displays, porosian excursions and exhibition shows.

附近交通: Shanghai Library Station on metro 10, Transport University Station on metro 11
景区介绍: Wukang Road, located in the Xu Seok district of Shanghai City, formerly known as Fukusen Road, was built in 1907 and is one of the most distinctive roads in Shanghai. There are numerous historical buildings, such as the Spanish-style, the French Renaissance House, the Bagin House, the Huang Xing Home, etc., which are referred to as the “Celebrity Road” which “enrichs the recent centuries of history in Shanghai”. The roads are shaded and filled with a rich old Shanghai wind, the best place to walk, take photographs and feel the historical culture of Shanghai.
适合人群: Historical and cultural lovers, photographers, leisure walkers
景区卖点: Historic old buildings, Celebrity former residences, French Concession charm, Tree-lined scenic road, Authentic Shanghai vibe

地址: 1686 Nanjing West Road, Jingan District, Shanghai
购票方式: On-site ticket purchase or online platform appointment
景区介绍: The Temple of Jing An is the famous ancient bastion of Shanghai, which began with the Sun Chuu Decade (247), and is one of the oldest Buddhist temples in Shanghai. The style of building in the temple combines Tibetan Buddhist and Han Buddhist features, with the main buildings being the Great Pagoda, the Temple of the King and the Three Temples. There are also valuable Buddhist monuments in the temple, such as the Buddha of Ming Dynasty and the Jade Buddha of Chiang Dyna.
适合人群: Religious cultural lovers, historical lovers, tourists
景区卖点: A millennium-old Buddhist temple, A peaceful oasis in downtown Shanghai, Unique fusion of Tibetan and Han Buddhist architecture, Home to precious ancient Buddhist relics, Centrally located with easy access
